title: citronella
intro: Citronella is a type of tropical grass, scientifically known as Cymbopogon nardus, native to tropical Asia and commonly used for its distinctive lemon-like scent. The medicinal properties of citronella include its ability to repel insects, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of citronella include its potential to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression, reduce fever, and soothe digestive issues. Citronella is often used in herbal preparations such as essential oils, candles, and potpourri, and can also be consumed as a tea to reap its medicinal benefits.
